meta.name: 10k Performance
meta.subtitle: Break 40 minutes — elite club target
meta.description: A 20-week advanced programme for competitive runners targeting sub-40 minutes for 10k. Built around VO2max intervals, lactate threshold runs, and aerobic volume, with two gym sessions per week for strength and injury prevention.
meta.coach_notes: Sub-40 for 10k requires 4:00/km for 10km. You need both a very high VO2max and exceptional lactate threshold. This plan attacks both. The interval sessions build the ceiling; the threshold runs raise the floor. Do not miss the threshold sessions — they are as important as the intervals.

weeks.title: Base establishment
weeks.note: Opening week for a serious 10k programme. Five running days, two quality sessions. Nothing maximal — establish your baseline and get the body used to the structure.

sessions.det: 7km easy · 5:45–6:10/km · True recovery pace — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

2km WU · 6x800m @ current 10k pace (2min rec) · 2km CD · 11km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

2km WU · 20min @ threshold (4:20–4:30/km) · 2km CD · 8km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Pull-ups 3x5 · DB row 3x10 · Push-ups 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x30s — Your first upper body session. Focus on form, not load. Upper body and core strength directly improves your running posture and arm drive efficiency.
8km easy · 6x100m strides built in — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
13km · 5:55–6:20/km · Aerobic base — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

weeks.title: VO2max introduction
weeks.note: First VO2max session. The 1km reps at 3:45–3:55/km are harder than 10k pace — they develop the aerobic ceiling that makes 4:00/km feel sustainable.

sessions.det: 7km easy · 5:45–6:10/km · True recovery pace — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 5x1km @ 3:45–3:55/km (3min jog rec) · 2km CD · 11km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

2km WU · 2x12min @ 4:20–4:30/km (90sec rec) · 2km CD · 9km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Back squat 3x10 · Romanian deadlift 3x10 · Bulgarian split squat 3x10 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.
9km easy · 6x100m strides built in — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

14km · 5:55–6:20/km · Aerobic base — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Volume build
weeks.note: Volume peaks in the first block. The long run reaches 16km. Keep the long run genuinely easy — this is aerobic base work, not race preparation.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:45–6:10/km · True recovery pace — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 6x1km @ 3:45–3:55/km (3min rec) · 2km CD · 12km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

2km WU · 2x15min @ 4:18–4:28/km (2min rec) · 2km CD · 10km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Pull-ups 3x10 · DB row 3x10 · Overhead press 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.
9km easy · 6x100m strides built in — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

16km · 5:55–6:20/km · Aerobic base — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Deload week
weeks.note: Deliberate recovery week. Volume drops by 35–40%. The fitness from the previous block consolidates now. Rest is the training instruction this week.

sessions.det: 6km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
6km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
5km easy · No targets — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
11km easy · Relaxed — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

weeks.title: Race pace introduction
weeks.note: Phase 2 of the base block introduces race-pace running. The 1200m reps at exactly 4:00/km build the specific endurance for your goal.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:45–6:10/km · True recovery pace — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 5x1200m @ 4:00/km (3min rec) · 2km CD · 12km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

2km WU · 25min @ 4:18–4:28/km · 2km CD · 9.5km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Pull-ups 3x10 · DB row 3x10 · Overhead press 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.
9km easy · 6x100m strides built in — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

15km · 5:55–6:20/km · Aerobic base — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Mixed quality
weeks.note: Mixed speed session. The combination of 800m and 400m reps in the same session trains your body to handle pace changes — essential for racing.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:45–6:10/km · True recovery pace — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 4x(800m @ 4:00/km, 2min rec, 400m @ 3:40/km, 90sec rec) · 2km CD · 11km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

2km WU · 30min @ 4:15–4:25/km · 2km CD · 11km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Back squat 3x10 · Romanian deadlift 3x10 · Bulgarian split squat 3x10 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.
9km easy · 6x100m strides built in — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

15km · 5:55–6:20/km · Aerobic base — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Volume peak 2
weeks.note: Second volume peak before the halfway deload. The long run has a race-pace section built in — it trains your body to run at goal pace under fatigue.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:45–6:10/km · True recovery pace — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 7x1km @ 3:45–3:55/km (3min rec) · 2km CD · 13km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

2km WU · 35min @ 4:15–4:25/km · 2km CD · 11.5km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Pull-ups 3x10 · DB row 3x10 · Overhead press 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

12km easy · Relaxed — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

weeks.title: Speed sharpening
weeks.note: Phase 2 opens with pure speed work. The 300m reps at mile pace train fast-twitch recruitment and make 4:00/km feel slow by comparison.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:45–6:10/km · True recovery pace — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 12x300m @ 3:25–3:35/km (90sec rec) · 2km CD · 9.5km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

2km WU · 30min @ 4:12–4:22/km · 2km CD · 11km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Pull-ups 3x10 · DB row 3x10 · Overhead press 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.
9km easy · 6x100m strides built in — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

15km · 5:55–6:20/km · Aerobic base — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Race simulation
weeks.note: The 8km time trial on Tuesday is the most important session of the plan. Run it at goal race pace — 4:00/km — and note exactly how it feels. This tells you whether your goal is realistic with 10 weeks remaining.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:45–6:10/km · True recovery pace — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 8km @ 4:00/km (race pace) · 1km CD · 11km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

2km WU · 2x16min @ 4:12–4:22/km (90sec rec) · 2km CD · 10km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Back squat 3x10 · Romanian deadlift 3x10 · Bulgarian split squat 3x10 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.
9km easy · 6x100m strides built in — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

15km · 5:55–6:20/km · Aerobic base — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Peak block 1
weeks.note: First peak week. The 4x1600m session is the cornerstone of the plan — 4 reps of 1600m at race pace develops the specific endurance to hold 4:00/km for 10km.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:45–6:10/km · True recovery pace — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km WU · 4x1600m @ 4:00–4:05/km (4min rec) · 2km CD · 13km total — Peak-phase intervals — this is where your fitness is tested. These are hard but controlled. If the pace slips, the session is done.

2km WU · 2x20min @ 4:12–4:22/km (2min rec) · 2km CD · 12km total — Threshold work at peak phase. Your lactate threshold determines the pace you can sustain for an hour. These sessions push that ceiling up.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.
9km easy · 6x100m strides built in — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

16km · 5:55–6:20/km · Aerobic base — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Peak block 2
weeks.note: Second peak week. Same sessions as last week — the repetition is intentional. Consistency builds fitness; variation breaks it.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:45–6:10/km · True recovery pace — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 5x1200m @ 3:52–4:00/km (3.5min rec) · 2km CD · 13km total — Peak-phase intervals — this is where your fitness is tested. These are hard but controlled. If the pace slips, the session is done.
2km WU · 35min @ 4:10–4:20/km · 2km CD · 12km total — Threshold work at peak phase. Your lactate threshold determines the pace you can sustain for an hour. These sessions push that ceiling up.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.
9km easy · 6x100m strides built in — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

16km · 5:55–6:20/km · Aerobic base — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Peak block 3
weeks.note: Third and final peak week. Race-pace 1km reps — confident, controlled, owning the pace. Three weeks until race day.

sessions.det: 7km easy · 5:45–6:10/km · True recovery pace — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 7x1km @ exactly 4:00/km (2.5min rec) · 2km CD · 13km total — Peak-phase intervals — this is where your fitness is tested. These are hard but controlled. If the pace slips, the session is done.
2km WU · 30min @ 4:10–4:20/km · 2km CD · 11km total — Threshold work at peak phase. Your lactate threshold determines the pace you can sustain for an hour. These sessions push that ceiling up.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.
8km easy · 6x100m strides built in — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

14km · 5:55–6:20/km · Aerobic base — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Taper — week 1
weeks.note: Taper begins. Volume drops, intensity stays. Your legs will feel sluggish then suddenly light — trust this process completely.

sessions.det: 6km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

1.5km WU · 6x600m @ race pace (2min rec) · 1.5km CD · 7.5km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

2km WU · 18min threshold · 2km CD · 8km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
6km easy with strides — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
11km easy · Comfortable — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

weeks.title: Taper — week 2
weeks.note: Second taper week. By Thursday you should feel genuinely good. One week to race day — stay calm and trust your training.

sessions.det: 5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

1.5km WU · 4x600m @ race pace (2min rec) · 1.5km CD · 6km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.
4km easy · Fresh and light — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
4km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
5km easy · Final prep run — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

weeks.title: Race week
weeks.note: Race week. Rest. Eat well. Sleep well. Everything this week serves Saturday's race. The fitness is built.

sessions.det: 3km easy · 4x100m strides at race pace — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
3km easy · Very light — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km easy · Stay loose — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km easy WU · 4x100m strides · Race: through 1km at 4:02, settle to 4:00, build from 7km · Negative split target — Race day. Trust your training. Start conservatively — most races are won or lost in the second half.

nut.d: Porridge with banana or toast with peanut butter. 500ml water. Only familiar foods.
200ml water. Warm up 15 minutes easy. Include strides if planned.
Take water at stations. For half marathon and above: gel at 6–7km and again at 11–12km if trained with gels.
Water immediately. 400–500ml in 10 minutes. Keep moving to cool down gently.
Full meal within 90 minutes. Protein, carbohydrates, everything. You have done something genuinely impressive.

weeks.title: Recovery
weeks.note: Race week overflow — if race is on Sunday of week 16, use this week for gentle recovery.

sessions.det: 4km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
6km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

weeks.title: Training week 17
weeks.note: Week 17 — progressive training continues.

sessions.det: 7km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
Standard interval session — Sharp intervals close to race week keep your legs feeling quick without adding volume. The fitness is already built — this is a reminder, not a workout.
Threshold run 25min — A short threshold session to maintain the feel of race effort. Comfortably hard — not racing.

Pull-ups 3x10 · DB row 3x10 · Overhead press 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.
8km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
14km easy — A confidence long run in taper — resist the urge to push. Arrive at the start line rested, not tired from a final fitness test.
